senses.definition: to establish or restore something, such as a system, policy, or organization, again after it has been stopped or withdrawn
examples: The government decided to reinstitute the program after a review.
They aim to reinstitute the traditional education methods in the new curriculum.

senses.definition: past tense of reinstitute; established or restored again
examples: The program was reinstituted last year.
After the budget approval, the policy was reinstituted.

senses.definition: third person singular of reinstitute; establishes or restores again
examples: The council reinstitutes the travel subsidy for students.
It reinstitutes the old examination format.

senses.definition: present participle of reinstitute; the act of establishing or restoring again
examples: The authorities are reinstituting the subsidy.
Reinstituting the policy requires careful planning.

senses.definition: the act or process of establishing something again that had been stopped or removed
examples: The reinstitution of the program took several months.
There was debate over the reinstitution of the policy.

senses.definition: the process of restoring or reorganizing an institution or system, especially after failure or reform, often used in social policy or mental health contexts
examples: The policy debate shifted toward reinstitutionalization of care facilities.
Critics warn against the risks of reinstitutionalization in social services.

senses.definition: plural of reinstitutionalization; multiple instances or processes of restoring institutions
examples: The report covered several reinstitutionalizations across regions.
Questions were raised about the effectiveness of the reinstitutionalizations.

senses.definition: to restore or establish something as a formal institution or system, often after being disbanded
examples: The plan aims to reinstitutionalize the public health system.
Experts warn against overzealous attempts to reinstitutionalize care.

senses.definition: past participle of reinstitutionalize; established again as an institution
examples: The services were reinstitutionalized after the reform.
Many facilities were reinstitutionalized under new management.

senses.definition: third person singular present of reinstitutionalize; establishes again as an institution
examples: The ministry reinstitutionalizes services to the public.
They reinstitutionalizes the program after the evaluation.

senses.definition: present participle of reinstitutionalize; the act of establishing again as an institution
examples: The government is reinstitutionalizing care services.
Reinstitutionalizing the system requires oversight.

senses.definition: to instruct again; to give new or improved instructions or training
examples: The trainer was hired to reinstruct the staff.
They will reinstruct the team on safety procedures.

senses.definition: past tense of reinstruct; gave new instructions or training
examples: The supervisor reinstructed the group on protocol.
They reinstructed employees after the incident.

senses.definition: present participle of reinstruct; the act of providing updated instructions or training
examples: They are reinstructing the staff for new procedures.
Reinstrucing the team can prevent errors.

senses.definition: the act of giving new or corrected instructions or training
examples: The reinstruction was completed quickly.
This reinstruction improves compliance.

senses.definition: third person singular present of reinstruct; gives new instructions or training
examples: The supervisor reinstructs the team after the drill.
He often reinstructs workers on safety rules.

senses.definition: to insulate again or further, often to improve thermal or acoustic insulation
examples: We had to reinsulate the attic to keep the house warm.
The contractor will reinsulate certain walls before finishing.

senses.definition: past tense of reinsulate; insulated again
examples: The pipes were reinsulated to prevent heat loss.
They reinsulated the ceiling after repairs.

senses.definition: present participle of reinsulate; the act of insulating again
examples: They are reinsulating the building as part of energy upgrades.
Reinsulating is recommended for older structures.

senses.definition: to insult again or in a repeated way
examples: Don't reinsult him just because he disagreed.
She felt they would reinsult her if she spoke up again.

senses.definition: insurance that is purchased by an insurance company to cover part of its risks
examples: The insurer bought reinsurance to manage risk.
Reinsurance helps insurers spread large losses.

senses.definition: plural of reinsurance; multiple reinsurance agreements or policies
examples: The company held several reinsurances to diversify risk.
Reinsurances include proportional and non-proportional types.

senses.definition: to provide insurance for an insurance company; buy reinsurance
examples: The company decided to reinsure part of its portfolio.
They will reinsure the risk with a major insurer.

senses.definition: past tense of reinsure; insured again or through reinsurance
examples: The risk was reinsured after the loss.
They were reinsured by a global insurer.

senses.definition: an insurance company that provides reinsurance to other insurers
examples: The reinsurer has a global network.
They negotiated coverage with a major reinsurer.
